dataset: BFI25.csv (derived from Revelle 2010 SAPA/IPIP BFI-25, psych R package, via vincentarelbundock/Rdatasets)

pi_items.source: Verified by reading /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/BFI25.csv in live environment. File exists, 2435 rows x 27 columns, all integers, no missing values. Derived from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vincentarelbundock/Rdatasets/master/csv/psych/bfi.csv by extract_bfi25.py in install_jamovi.sh. Original dataset is Revelle (2010) SAPA project with 2800 subjects; after dropping rows with NAs and out-of-range values, 2435 remain.
Confirmed file exists at this exact path in live environment via SSH: 'ls -la /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/BFI25.csv' returned 75143 bytes, owned by ga:ga.
Verified that directory /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/ exists and is writable by user ga. setup_task.sh correctly removes any pre-existing file at this path before the task starts.
Verified that directory exists and is writable. setup_task.sh correctly removes any pre-existing file at this path.

Confirmed via R psych package documentation (https://search.r-project.org/CRAN/refmans/psych/html/bfi.html) and Rdatasets doc (https://vincentarelbundock.github.io/Rdatasets/doc/psych/bfi.html). BFI scoring keys use -E1 and -E2 for Extraversion scale. E1='Don't talk a lot', E2='Find it difficult to approach others' — both negatively worded, requiring reverse coding. The correct reverse formula on a 1-6 scale is 7 - original.

Confirmed via psych package documentation. Extraversion scale = {-E1, -E2, E3, E4, E5} where minus means reverse-coded. After computing E1r=7-E1 and E2r=7-E2, the items are E1r, E2r, E3, E4, E5. Columns E3, E4, E5 verified present in BFI25.csv with range [1,6].
Confirmed via psych package documentation. Neuroticism scale = {N1, N2, N3, N4, N5} — all forward-coded (no reverse coding needed). All five columns verified present in BFI25.csv with range [1,6].

These are the 4 computed variables the agent must create: E1r=7-E1, E2r=7-E2, Extra=MEAN(E1r,E2r,E3,E4,E5), Neuro=MEAN(N1,N2,N3,N4,N5). Verified by computing them in Python on the actual data. Extraversion mean=4.1301 SD=1.0677; Neuroticism mean=3.1717 SD=1.2004.
Column 'age' confirmed present in BFI25.csv. Range: 11-86, mean=28.5852, SD=10.9523. All integer values, no missing data.
Column 'gender' confirmed present in BFI25.csv. Coded as integers: 1=Male (N=804), 2=Female (N=1631). Must be set to nominal/factor in Jamovi before use in regression.

Verified that hierarchical regression with Block 1 (gender) and Block 2 (gender + Extra + Neuro) can be computed. Ran the full regression in Python/statsmodels on actual data. Model converges, all predictors have valid coefficients.

All 13 report fields independently computed from the actual BFI25.csv data using Python/statsmodels/scipy. Ground truth values: extra_alpha=0.7653, block1_r2=0.0015, block2_r2=0.0174, r2_change=0.0158, model_f=14.33, model_p<0.001, beta_extra=0.043, beta_neuro=-0.109, vif_extra=1.078, vif_neuro=1.084, durbin_watson=1.836, shapiro_w=0.917, shapiro_p<0.001. Note: Jamovi values may differ slightly from Python/statsmodels due to different computation engines (Jamovi uses R internally), but should be very close.

notes.computation_method: Python 3.13 with pandas, statsmodels 0.14, scipy. Gender treated as categorical factor (C(gender)) with reference coding. Standardized betas computed by z-scoring age, Extra, Neuro before fitting. VIF computed from OLS regression of each predictor on others with constant.
notes.jamovi_differences: Jamovi uses R-based jmv engine internally. R-squared, F-statistic, Durbin-Watson, and Shapiro-Wilk should match very closely. Standardized betas may differ by ~0.01 depending on Jamovi's standardization method. Cronbach's alpha should match to 3 decimal places.
notes.strongest_predictor: Neuroticism has the largest absolute standardized beta (|−0.109| > |0.043|). Neuroticism is a NEGATIVE predictor of age (younger participants report higher neuroticism).
notes.residual_normality: Shapiro-Wilk strongly rejects normality (p ≈ 1.3e-34). This is expected with N=2435 — large samples almost always fail Shapiro-Wilk even with approximately normal distributions.
notes.multicollinearity: VIF values near 1.08 indicate virtually no multicollinearity between Extraversion and Neuroticism.
notes.model_significance: The full model (Block 2) is highly significant (p ≈ 2.9e-09) but explains only ~1.7% of age variance. This is typical for personality-demographics relationships.
notes.dataset_details: 2435 rows, 27 columns (A1-A5, C1-C5, E1-E5, N1-N5, O1-O5, gender, age). All integers. Gender: 1=Male (N=804), 2=Female (N=1631). Age: 11-86 (mean=28.59, SD=10.95). No missing values. Items on 1-6 Likert scale.

privileged_info_summary: The BFI25.csv dataset contains 2435 complete cases with 27 columns (25 personality items A1-O5 on 1-6 Likert scale, plus gender coded 1=Male/2=Female, and age ranging 11-86). Items E1 and E2 are negatively worded Extraversion items requiring reverse coding via 7 minus original score. The Extraversion scale (E1r, E2r, E3, E4, E5) has Cronbach's alpha approximately 0.765. In a hierarchical linear regression predicting age: Block 1 (gender only) yields R-squared approximately 0.0015; Block 2 (gender + Extraversion + Neuroticism) yields R-squared approximately 0.0174 with R-squared change approximately 0.016. The overall model F-statistic for Block 2 is approximately 14.3 with p-value less than 0.001. Standardized beta for Extraversion is approximately 0.043 (positive, small) and for Neuroticism approximately -0.109 (negative, larger). Neuroticism is the strongest personality predictor of age. VIF values are approximately 1.08 for both predictors, indicating no multicollinearity. Durbin-Watson d is approximately 1.84, close to 2, indicating no autocorrelation. Shapiro-Wilk W for residuals is approximately 0.917 with p-value far below 0.001, meaning residuals are NOT normally distributed (expected with N=2435). The agent must: compute 4 variables (E1r, E2r, Extra, Neuro), run 1 reliability analysis, set up hierarchical regression with 2 blocks and multiple diagnostic options (standardized estimates, CIs, VIF, Durbin-Watson), save residuals to data, run Shapiro-Wilk on residuals, save .omv file, and write a report with 13 specific numeric values.
